sources.title: Gains Product Specification
principles: Show the summary first and let deeper detail unfold progressively.
Keep the primary action centered around fast stock search and immediate decision support.

sources.title: Stock Research Playbook
principles: Separate business quality, valuation, and timing so the decision framework stays honest.
Distinguish confirmed market data from inference and scenario planning.

sources.title: The Intelligent Investor
principles: Require a margin of safety before treating a stock as a high-conviction investment.
Treat preservation of capital as important as upside.

sources.title: Common Stocks and Uncommon Profits
principles: Management quality and durable competitive advantage should influence long-term conviction.
Strong businesses deserve a different lens than short-term trades.

sources.title: One Up On Wall Street
principles: Use understandable businesses and runway as a filter for multi-year ideas.
Look for practical edge, not abstract complexity.

sources.title: Mastering the Trade
principles: A swing setup needs a defined trigger, stop, and reward profile.
Momentum without risk definition is not enough.

sources.title: Japanese Candlestick Charting Techniques
principles: Price structure and reversal confirmation should support the trade idea.
Single candles are weaker than structure plus confirmation.

sources.title: Technical Analysis of the Financial Markets
principles: Trend, momentum, and volume should be read together rather than in isolation.
Support and resistance matter most when multiple signals align.

sources.title: A Random Walk Down Wall Street
principles: Diversification and benchmark awareness should influence portfolio design.
Be skeptical of overconfident forecasts.

sources.title: Reminiscences of a Stock Operator
principles: Respect market behavior and avoid forcing trades.
Position sizing and patience matter as much as the idea itself.
